commands.review_summary:
The PR scopes Control UI assistant-avatar localStorage reads and writes with `openclaw.control.assistant.v1:<agentId>` keys and adds a legacy global-key fallback.

PR surface: Source +16. Total +16 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.1 use one global assistant-avatar localStorage key, and the linked issue gives direct switch-agent steps. I did not run a live browser reproduction because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer revision plus real UI proof; missing external proof makes this a human-only merge blocker rather than a repair marker lane.

target.title: fix(ui): scope avatar storage per agent ID
target.body:
fix(ui): scope avatar storage per agent ID

## Summary
**Problem:** The avatar storage used a single global localStorage key `openclaw.control.assistant.v1`, causing setting an avatar for one agent to overwrite it for all agents.

**Fix:** Scope the localStorage key per agent ID: `openclaw.control.assistant.v1:<agentId>`. Include backward compatibility: existing global avatars are used as fallback when no agent-specific avatar exists.

**Out of scope:** No changes to avatar upload, display, or server-side storage.

## Linked context
Closes #90890

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Setting avatar for one agent changes it for all agents
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js v22.11.0, OpenClaw latest main
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Verified the localStorage key is scoped per agent ID
- Evidence after fix: Code change in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`:
```typescript
// Before:
const LOCAL_ASSISTANT_IDENTITY_KEY = "openclaw.control.assistant.v1";

// After:
const LOCAL_ASSISTANT_IDENTITY_KEY_PREFIX = "openclaw.control.assistant.v1";

function localAssistantIdentityKey(agentId?: string): string {
  const id = agentId?.trim();
  return id ? `${LOCAL_ASSISTANT_IDENTITY_KEY_PREFIX}:${id}` : LOCAL_ASSISTANT_IDENTITY_KEY_PREFIX;
}
```
- Observed result after fix: Each agent now has its own avatar stored separately
- What was not tested: Live multi-agent avatar test (requires multiple agents)

## Tests and validation
- Existing tests in `ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.test.ts` cover the basic path
- No CHANGELOG.md edits

## Risk checklist
- userVisible: Yes (avatars now scoped per agent)
- configEnvMigration: No (backward compatible with global key)
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Backward compatible; existing global avatars used as fallback

## Current review state
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
